reforms.description: The recent creation of the National Science Fund and the Research and Technology Fund aims to enhance the responsiveness of public-sector research to business and societal needs by improving the allocation of resources between programmes and research areas. Additionally, the 'Innovation Plan' announced in April 2003 aims to increase total R&D spending from 2.2% of GDP to 3% by 2010, with a focus on boosting private sector R&D investment.
reforms.component_name: National Science Fund and Research and Technology Fund
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to increase R&D spending and improve resource allocation, which are expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Science Fund and Research and Technology Fund have been established and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of national funds and a comprehensive innovation plan represent significant structural changes in R&D funding.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Innovation Plan' announced in April 2003 hopes to raise total Research and Development (R&D) spending from its current level of 2.2 per cent of GDP to 3 per cent by 2010.

reforms.description: The government has introduced measures to exonerate young and innovative firms from corporate income tax for their first three profitable years and provide various tax exemptions to encourage R&D investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These tax incentives are designed to stimulate R&D investment among startups, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been announced but may not yet be fully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of significant tax exemptions for innovative firms represents a major reform in innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: The creation and survival of young and innovative firms include exonerating such enterprises from corporate income tax for their first three profitable years.

reforms.description: The French government announced the creation of a State Ownership Agency (APE) to oversee state-owned companies and ensure they operate on commercial principles, addressing governance issues and enhancing transparency.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the agency on innovation is uncertain as it primarily focuses on governance rather than direct innovation support.
reforms.status_evidence: The agency has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the establishment of the APE is significant, it does not represent a major reform in innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have taken up one recommendation by announcing the creation of a State Ownership Agency (Agence des participations de l’État, APE), which will be charged with watching over the State’s holdings.

reforms.description: France has introduced measures to increase incentives for innovation, including the establishment of a network of industrialists and research laboratories to foster collaboration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of a collaborative network is expected to enhance innovation through improved partnerships between industry and research.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put into practice and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the measures represent a narrower reform compared to larger funding initiatives.
reforms.source_quote: Efforts to stimulate R&D. Measure have been undertaken to increase incentives for innovation, including the establishment of network of industrialists and research laboratories.
